title: Multi-point functions of a full-plane two-state fuzzy $4$-Potts model
abstract: We study the full-plane two-state fuzzy $4$-Potts model, obtained by assigning independent balanced $\{\pm1\}$ spins to the open clusters of a critical $q=4$ random-cluster configuration. This model corresponds exactly to the single-spin projection of the isotropic Ashkin-Teller model at its Potts point. We prove that, after proper normalization, all even multi-point spin correlation functions converge to explicit conformally covariant Coulomb-gas type neutral charge sums. As a consequence, we prove convergence in law of the rescaled magnetization field and identify the moments of the limiting field. The proof combines the Baxter-Kelland-Wu coupling, convergence of the six-vertex height function to the Gaussian free field, and a charge-completion mechanism: an enlarged discrete sum over charge assignments with total charge in $4\mathbb Z$ produces a combinatorial cancellation of connection patterns, while only the neutral charge sector survives in the scaling limit.
summary: This theoretical paper analyzes the critical behavior of the fuzzy 4-Potts model on the plane. It proves that the model's multi-point correlation functions converge to explicit, conformally covariant forms characteristic of Coulomb gas theories. The work establishes the continuum limit of the magnetization field, providing deep insights into the scaling properties of lattice statistical models near criticality.
main_problem: The study focuses on determining the scaling limits of multi-point spin correlation functions for the full-plane two-state fuzzy 4-Potts model.
main_result: The even multi-point spin correlation functions converge to explicit conformally covariant Coulomb-gas type neutral charge sums, and the rescaled magnetization field converges in law to a continuum field $\Phi_0$.
method: The proof combines advanced techniques including the Baxter-Kelland-Wu coupling, the convergence of the six-vertex height function to the Gaussian Free Field (GFF), and a rigorous charge-completion mechanism.
model_or_system: The model is the full-plane two-state fuzzy 4-Potts model, derived from assigning spins to open clusters of a critical q=4 random-cluster configuration, which is equivalent to the single-spin projection of the isotropic Ashkin-Teller model at its Potts point.
key_observables: Multi-point spin correlation functions, the rescaled magnetization field ($\Phi_\delta$), and their limiting moments ($M_n(h)$).
important_parameters: The critical point ($p_c = 2/3$), the scaling factor $f(\delta) \sim \delta^{1/8}$, and the limit $\delta 	o 0$ (the continuum limit).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the convergence of the six-vertex height function to the GFF and the ability to establish convergence in law via moment matching in Sobolev spaces.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by first establishing the convergence of even multi-point functions to a Coulomb-gas sum (Theorem 2.2), then defining the continuum limit of the magnetization field ($\Phi_\delta 	o \Phi_0$ in $H^{-s}$), and finally completing the proof by deriving necessary combinatorial and scaling relations (A, B, C, D) to confirm the final scaling limit (Proposition 3.1).
why_interesting: The rigorous connection between lattice statistical mechanics models (like the Potts model) and continuous field theories (like the GFF/CFT) is fundamental to understanding critical phenomena in many physical systems, including those relevant to quantum many-body physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
